kp5:1.2: at one time the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
kp5:1.3:
Then a certain deva, in the far extreme of the night,
her extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One.
On arrival, having bowed down to him, she stood to one side.
kp5:1.4: As she was standing there, she addressed him with a verse.
kp5:2.4:
Tell, then, the highest protection.”
The Buddha:
kp5:3.3: paying homage to those worthy of homage:
kp5:4.2: having made merit in the past,
kp5:6.2: assistance to one’s wife and children,
kp5:8.3: being heedful of the mental qualities
kp5:9.3: hearing the Dhamma on timely occasions:
kp5:10.3: discussing the Dhamma on timely occasions:
kp5:12.1: A mind that, when touched by the ways of the world,
kp5:12.2: is unshaken, sorrowless, dustless, at rest:
